content.content:
1	# GSD-T Progress
2	
3	## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
4	## Status: M43 PARTITIONED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (target v3.17.10)
5	## Date: 2026-04-21
6	## Version: 3.16.12
7	
8	## Current Milestone
9	
10	**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.
11	
12	**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.
13	
14	**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.
15	
16	**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
17	- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
18	- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
19	- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).
20	
21	**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
22	- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
23	- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
24	- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.
25	
26	**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.
27	
28	**Out of scope (deferred)**:
29	- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
30	- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 PARTITIONED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (target v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.16.12

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).

content.content:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 PARTITIONED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (target v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.16.12

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

Full 3-run verdict remains an operator action (user invokes `gsd-t benchmark-orchestrator --runs 3` with real auth). **Wiring**: `bin/gsd-t.js` help now lists `benchmark-orchestrator`; the subcommand case spawnSyncs the driver with forwarded args (mirrors `orchestrate`). README documents workload shape, invariants, and why 4 tasks / 2 waves is the right size (enough to exercise the orchestrator, small enough that orchestration overhead dominates model-throughput noise). 5/5 smoke tests pass (test/m40-benchmark-workload-fixture.test.js): layout present, `tasks.md` parses cleanly via D1 queue (4 tasks / 2 waves, no forward cross-wave deps), `buildTaskBrief` produces ≤10 KB briefs for all 4 tasks with Task + Done Signal sections. Full suite: **1323/1323** (was 1318, +5). **D0-T2 (benchmark driver) is next** but is intentionally operator-run — the driver spawns real `claude -p` processes to compare orchestrator vs in-session wall-clock, which requires live Claude auth and 3×N×~30 s of real workload per run. The driver skeleton + smoke-test wrapper (D0-T2 + D0-T3) can be built in-session; the PASS/FAIL verdict (`BENCHMARK: PASS|FAIL` → written to `docs/m40-benchmark-report.md`) comes from an operator running `gsd-t benchmark-orchestrator --runs 3`. If the verdict FAILs, the M40 plan is to halt before D4/D5.
- 2026-04-19 19:30: [execute] M40 Wave 0 — D1 Tasks 4 + 5 landed (main orchestrator CLI + `gsd-t orchestrate` subcommand). **D1-T4** (`bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js`): exports `runOrchestrator({projectDir, milestone, maxParallel?, workerTimeoutMs?, logger?, spawnImpl?, runWorkerImpl?})`, `runWaveTasks`, `parseCliArgs`, `atomicWriteJson`. Dep parser handles `NONE`/`N/A`/empty, same-domain `Task N` → `domain:TN`, and cross-domain `BLOCKED BY d3-completion-protocol Task 2` → `d3-completion-protocol:T2`. Smoke test on real M40 tree: 30 tasks across 7 domains, Wave 0=14, Wave 2=1, Wave 3=11, Wave 4=4, dep validation passes. 13/13 unit tests pass (test/m40-orchestrator-queue.test.js). Full suite: 1272/1272 (was 1259, +13). D1 Task 3 (worker lifecycle — consumes `assertCompletion` from D3-T2) next.

Defaults: maxParallel=3, workerTimeoutMs=270000, retryOnFail=true, haltOnSecondFail=true. Rejects maxParallel > 15 (Team Mode §15 ceiling), maxParallel < 1, workerTimeoutMs < 1000, non-integer env values, corrupt config file. 11/11 unit tests pass (test/m40-orchestrator-config.test.js). Full suite: 1259/1259. D1 Tasks 2-3 (queue, worker) next.

Downstream D1/D2 unblocked to cite the frozen contract. D3 Task 2 (assertCompletion impl) next.
- 2026-04-19 18:00: [milestone-planned] M40 PLANNED — 28 tasks across 7 domains. Wave 0 (foundation+gate): 15 tasks in 5 sub-groups (0a independent starts: D3-T1, D1-T1, D2-T1, D0-T1; 0b same-domain unlock; 0c cross-domain integration; 0d orchestrator CLI ready; 0e THE GATE = D0-T2 benchmark driver → D0-T3 verdict). Wave 2 (after D0 PASS): D1-T6 full worker. Wave 3 (stream feed): D4 Tasks 1-5 + D5 Tasks 1-5 + D1-T7 stream wiring. Wave 4 (recovery): D6 Tasks 1-4. REQ traceability appended to `docs/requirements.md` with 5 M40 REQs (REQ-M40-01 through REQ-M40-05), all mapped to tasks. Scope-validation: no task >5 files, no cross-domain dep >3 per task. No duplicate operations across domains (D1/D2/D3 roles are orthogonal; D4/D5 split by transport vs rendering). Tests baseline 1240/1240 pass. Ready for Wave 0 execute.
- 2026-04-19 17:45: [milestone-partitioned] M40 PARTITIONED into 7 domains across 5 waves. D0 speed-benchmark (KILL-SWITCH GATE — Wave 0), D1 orchestrator-core, D2 task-brief-builder, D3 completion-protocol (contract-only, lands first), D4 stream-feed-server, D5 stream-feed-ui, D6 recovery-and-resume. Wave 0 assembles D3 contract + D1/D2 minimal slices + D0 benchmark run — if benchmark FAIL, milestone halts before D4/D5 ever start (per user: "It's not worth doing if it doesn't run at least as fast[er]"). 4 new contracts written: task-brief-contract.md v1.0.0, completion-signal-contract.md v1.0.0, stream-json-sink-contract.md v1.0.0, wave-join-contract.md v1.0.0. Existing code dispositions locked in: `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs` INSPECT, existing events JSONL USE, `scripts/gsd-t-agent-dashboard*` (untracked, 424+1043 LOC) INSPECT-then-decide for D4/D5 promotion-or-rewrite, `bin/model-selector.js` + `bin/token-budget.cjs` NOT USED inside workers (one-shot workers don't need context meter), `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` UNCHANGED, unattended supervisor UNCHANGED. Five ambiguity-locks recorded (claude.ai-style = visual conventions not clone; streaming = replay+tail; zero-token = no LLM at all; parallelism = child_process.spawn; recovery = operator-initiated not auto). Baseline tests: 1240/1240 pass. Domains ready for `/gsd-t-plan` to fill tasks.md files.
- 2026-04-19 11:00: [milestone-defined] M40 — External Task Orchestrator + Streaming Watcher UI committed to progress.md. Target v3.14.10 (minor bump, new major feature). Rationale: M8-sized work exceeds single-session context; in-session compaction loses cross-file reasoning; unattended is 5–10× slower with 5-min visibility gap. Solution: external node orchestrator spawns short-lived `claude -p` workers (one task per spawn = no compaction possible), pipes stream-json to a dumb local watcher UI (`localhost:7842`) that renders claude.ai-style continuous feed at zero Claude token cost. Per-wave Promise.all parallelism (default 3, max 15 per Team Mode §15) — true process-level, not subagent-level. D0 speed-benchmark gate built first: if orchestrator ≯ in-session on same domain, kill M40 before D4/D5 UI work. Seven proposed domains (D0–D6) — partition will validate/adjust. Ready for partition.

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 VERIFIED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (ready for complete-milestone, target v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.16.12

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

## Milestones

| # | Milestone | Status | Version | Domains |
|---|-----------|--------|---------|---------|
| M43 | Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion | PARTITIONED | 3.17.10 (target) | 6 domains across 2 themes, 3 waves (W1 D3+D1 foundation · W2 D2+D5+D6 parallel · W3 D4 alone). **Part A — Universal Token Attribution**: D1 per-turn dialog-channel usage capture (hook-based if Claude Code exposes usage on Stop/SessionEnd — probe shipped `f94857b` — else extend M42 transcript tee to interactive); D2 per-tool attribution (`bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins events JSONL + token-usage JSONL by `turn_id`, attributes by output-byte ratio; `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain`); D3 sink unification + backfill (canonical schema v2, `token-log.md` regenerated view, backfill 64 historical Tokens=0 rows). **Part B — Always-Headless Inversion (channel separation)**: D4 strip headless/in-session branching from ~14 command files — every command spawns, no `--in-session` flag, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark; D5 collapses from compaction-pressure circuit breaker to a router-only dialog meter (warn on growth; never refuses/reroutes); D6 transcript viewer (`:7433`) as **the** primary surface — URL printed at every spawn, dashboard auto-launches if not running. tool_use_result.originalFile:
# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 VERIFIED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (ready for complete-milestone, target v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.16.12

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

Dep parser handles `NONE`/`N/A`/empty, same-domain `Task N` → `domain:TN`, and cross-domain `BLOCKED BY d3-completion-protocol Task 2` → `d3-completion-protocol:T2`. Smoke test on real M40 tree: 30 tasks across 7 domains, Wave 0=14, Wave 2=1, Wave 3=11, Wave 4=4, dep validation passes. 13/13 unit tests pass (test/m40-orchestrator-queue.test.js). Defaults: maxParallel=3, workerTimeoutMs=270000, retryOnFail=true, haltOnSecondFail=true. Rejects maxParallel > 15 (Team Mode §15 ceiling), maxParallel < 1, workerTimeoutMs < 1000, non-integer env values, corrupt config file. 11/11 unit tests pass (test/m40-orchestrator-config.test.js). Full suite: 1259/1259. D1 Tasks 2-3 (queue, worker) next.

Downstream D1/D2 unblocked to cite the frozen contract. D3 Task 2 (assertCompletion impl) next.

REQ traceability appended to `docs/requirements.md` with 5 M40 REQs (REQ-M40-01 through REQ-M40-05), all mapped to tasks. Scope-validation: no task >5 files, no cross-domain dep >3 per task. No duplicate operations across domains (D1/D2/D3 roles are orthogonal; D4/D5 split by transport vs rendering). Tests baseline 1240/1240 pass. Ready for Wave 0 execute.

D0 speed-benchmark (KILL-SWITCH GATE — Wave 0), D1 orchestrator-core, D2 task-brief-builder, D3 completion-protocol (contract-only, lands first), D4 stream-feed-server, D5 stream-feed-ui, D6 recovery-and-resume. Wave 0 assembles D3 contract + D1/D2 minimal slices + D0 benchmark run — if benchmark FAIL, milestone halts before D4/D5 ever start (per user: "It's not worth doing if it doesn't run at least as fast[er]"). 4 new contracts written: task-brief-contract.md v1.0.0, completion-signal-contract.md v1.0.0, stream-json-sink-contract.md v1.0.0, wave-join-contract.md v1.0.0. Existing code dispositions locked in: `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs` INSPECT, existing events JSONL USE, `scripts/gsd-t-agent-dashboard*` (untracked, 424+1043 LOC) INSPECT-then-decide for D4/D5 promotion-or-rewrite, `bin/model-selector.js` + `bin/token-budget.cjs` NOT USED inside workers (one-shot workers don't need context meter), `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` UNCHANGED, unattended supervisor UNCHANGED. Five ambiguity-locks recorded (claude.ai-style = visual conventions not clone; streaming = replay+tail; zero-token = no LLM at all; parallelism = child_process.spawn; recovery = operator-initiated not auto). Baseline tests: 1240/1240 pass. Domains ready for `/gsd-t-plan` to fill tasks.md files.

Rationale: M8-sized work exceeds single-session context; in-session compaction loses cross-file reasoning; unattended is 5–10× slower with 5-min visibility gap. Solution: external node orchestrator spawns short-lived `claude -p` workers (one task per spawn = no compaction possible), pipes stream-json to a dumb local watcher UI (`localhost:7842`) that renders claude.ai-style continuous feed at zero Claude token cost. # GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 COMPLETED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.17.10

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

## Milestones

Contracts: metrics-schema v1→v2 (drops `compaction_pressure` field from original sketch), stream-json-sink v1.1.0→v1.2.0, new tool-attribution-contract, headless-default v1.0.0→v2.0.0 (**deletes** `--in-session` opt-out, documents channel-separation invariant). |

## Decision Log

> Prior decision log entries preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/*/progress.md` — see archive snapshots (most recently `M40-external-task-orchestrator-2026-04-20/progress.md`) for pre-M40 history.

- 2026-04-21 17:21: [execute M43 D4 W3] always-headless inversion (channel separation) — `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` + `.js`: `shouldSpawnHeadless` exported as constant `() => true`, removed the `watch=true + spawnType=='primary' → {mode: 'in-context'}` early-return branch, legacy `watch`/`inSession` params accepted-and-ignored with one-shot stderr deprecation warning. `autoSpawnHeadless` now returns `mode: 'headless'` for all four propagation-matrix rows. Contract `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` bumped v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 with §Invariants, §What Was Deleted, §Migration, §Version History; D6 live-transcript URL banner + dashboard autostart preserved verbatim. Tests: `test/headless-default.test.js` propagation matrix flipped to all-headless; `test/watch-unattended-integration.test.js::auto_spawn_watch_primary_spawns_headless_flag_ignored_v2` asserts `MODE=headless`; NEW `test/m43-headless-default-inversion.test.js` (40 tests: `shouldSpawnHeadless` always-true across 9×3×3 pct/watch/inSession matrix, `autoSpawnHeadless` `mode: 'headless'` for all 4 matrix cells + legacy `inSession`, 14-command-file grep-assertion for zero operational `--in-session`/`--headless`/`WATCH_FLAG`, `/gsd` router surface checks for D5 footer + v2.0.0 banner + no operational removed flags). Doc ripple: `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` + `templates/CLAUDE-project.md` + `README.md` (Headless + Context Meter sections). `GSD-T-README.md` does not exist in this repo. Full suite: **1748/1750 pass** (2 pre-existing fails unrelated: `buildEventStreamEntry` + `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id`). Next: Wave 3 closes with this commit; M43 ready for verify + complete-milestone.

Fix: `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js` computes `canonicalTaskId` once (`task.id.includes(':T')` → `${domain}-t${N}`), threads it through the brief AND a `taskForWorker = {...task, canonicalId}`; `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-worker.cjs` now passes `task.canonicalId || task.id` into `assertCompletion`. **Bug 2** (ownedPatterns never plumbed through): `task.ownedPatterns` was always `[]`; completion-check's `matchesAny` returns true when patterns empty, so every uncommitted file counted as uncommitted_owned_changes — instant fail. Fix: `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-queue.cjs` new `parseFilesField` function extracts backtick-quoted paths from a task's `**Files**:` line (strips `(NEW)`/`(UPDATED)` suffixes, rewrites trailing-slash to `/**` globs, comma-split fallback); `parseTasksFile` now populates `task.ownedPatterns` from that field. 5 new queue tests added (`test/m40-orchestrator-queue.test.js`: parseFilesField backtick extraction, trailing-slash glob, comma-split fallback, NONE/N/A → [], parseTasksFile ownedPatterns population + absent-Files → []). **Bug 3** (fixture byproducts counted as uncommitted): orchestrator writes `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl`, `.gsd-t/orchestrator/state.json`, context-meter state, and heartbeats inside the workload dir during a run. All appear as uncommitted files; with Bug 2 fixed via real ownedPatterns, they now correctly stay outside a task's owned patterns — but the workload itself needed a `.gitignore` so a clean fixture doesn't fail its own completion check, and needed a real `package.json` + `test/run.js` so worker test runs don't invent one. Fix: `test/fixtures/m40-benchmark-workload/.gitignore` (excludes `.gsd-t/events/`, `.gsd-t/orchestrator/`, `.gsd-t/.context-meter-state.json`, `.gsd-t/context-meter.log`, `.gsd-t/heartbeat-*.jsonl`, `node_modules/`); `package.json` with `"test": "node test/run.js"`; `test/run.js` minimal tolerant runner (iterates `test/bench/*.test.js`, tolerates missing dir / empty set — workload is pre-wave-0 valid). **Fixture tests extended**: `test/m40-benchmark-workload-fixture.test.js` +3 existence assertions (package.json, test/run.js, .gitignore), +1 `.gitignore` content test, +1 ownedPatterns plumbing test. Full suite: **1349/1349** (was 1340, +9). Next operator action: re-run `node bin/gsd-t-benchmark-orchestrator.js --keep-tmp --runs 1` for trustworthy verdict; if orch state.json now shows `status: done` with no `missing` entries per task, the completion-check path is verified and a full `--runs 3` run can produce the real PASS/FAIL gate decision.

First verdict data — orch median 90844ms vs insession 62011ms, all 3 orch runs exit=1, all 3 insession runs exit=0 — so the speed delta (1.46x slower) is real but confounded by orchestrator hard-failing before workload completes. Root cause: `runOrchestratorSide`/`runInsessionSide` captured only `res.stderr`; orchestrator logs `[wave_halt]` via `logger.log` which goes to stdout, so the diagnostic signal was silently dropped. Fix (commit TBD): new `captureOutput()` helper labels and concatenates stdout + stderr into the `stderr` field of each run record (last 4KB). New `--keep-tmp` CLI flag preserves per-run tmp dirs (previously auto-wiped) so operators can inspect `.gsd-t/orchestrator/state.json`, `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, and `git log` of a failing run. `runBenchmark` now threads `keepTmp` through to both side runners; each run record gains a `tmpDir` field (null when keepTmp=false). Tests: +4 coverage (parseCliArgs --keep-tmp, captureOutput regression, keepTmp=true preserves + keepTmp=false cleans). Full suite 1340/1340 (was 1336). NOT recording `[benchmark-gate] FAIL` in progress.md — data is untrustworthy until stdout capture lands. Next operator action: re-run `node bin/gsd-t-benchmark-orchestrator.js --keep-tmp --runs 1` for one diagnostic run; inspect the preserved tmp dir + new `stderr` field in benchmark-results.json to identify the real failure mode (assertCompletion strictness vs worker timeout vs legit overhead).

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M43 COMPLETED — Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion (v3.17.10)
## Date: 2026-04-21
## Version: 3.17.10

## Current Milestone

**M43 Token Attribution & Always-Headless Inversion** — DEFINED (6 domains). Target: v3.17.10.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

## Milestones

Contracts: metrics-schema v1→v2 (drops `compaction_pressure` field from original sketch), stream-json-sink v1.1.0→v1.2.0, new tool-attribution-contract, headless-default v1.0.0→v2.0.0 (**deletes** `--in-session` opt-out, documents channel-separation invariant). |

## Decision Log

> Prior decision log entries preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/*/progress.md` — see archive snapshots (most recently `M40-external-task-orchestrator-2026-04-20/progress.md`) for pre-M40 history.

- 2026-04-21 17:21: [execute M43 D4 W3] always-headless inversion (channel separation) — `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` + `.js`: `shouldSpawnHeadless` exported as constant `() => true`, removed the `watch=true + spawnType=='primary' → {mode: 'in-context'}` early-return branch, legacy `watch`/`inSession` params accepted-and-ignored with one-shot stderr deprecation warning. `autoSpawnHeadless` now returns `mode: 'headless'` for all four propagation-matrix rows. Contract `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` bumped v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 with §Invariants, §What Was Deleted, §Migration, §Version History; D6 live-transcript URL banner + dashboard autostart preserved verbatim. Tests: `test/headless-default.test.js` propagation matrix flipped to all-headless; `test/watch-unattended-integration.test.js::auto_spawn_watch_primary_spawns_headless_flag_ignored_v2` asserts `MODE=headless`; NEW `test/m43-headless-default-inversion.test.js` (40 tests: `shouldSpawnHeadless` always-true across 9×3×3 pct/watch/inSession matrix, `autoSpawnHeadless` `mode: 'headless'` for all 4 matrix cells + legacy `inSession`, 14-command-file grep-assertion for zero operational `--in-session`/`--headless`/`WATCH_FLAG`, `/gsd` router surface checks for D5 footer + v2.0.0 banner + no operational removed flags). Doc ripple: `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` + `templates/CLAUDE-project.md` + `README.md` (Headless + Context Meter sections). `GSD-T-README.md` does not exist in this repo. Full suite: **1748/1750 pass** (2 pre-existing fails unrelated: `buildEventStreamEntry` + `writer_shim_safe_empty_agent_id_auto_mints_id`). Next: Wave 3 closes with this commit; M43 ready for verify + complete-milestone.

- 2026-04-21 21:10: [execute M43 D6 W2] transcript viewer as primary surface — D6-T1..T6 all DONE. **D6-T1**: `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` gains two new routes. `GET /transcript/:spawnId/usage` → `readSpawnUsageRows` reads `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` and filters rows where `row.spawn_id === spawnId` OR (no `spawn_id` column AND `row.session_id === spawnId`) — the latter covers D1 Branch B in-session rows that carry only a session id. Response `{spawn_id, rows, truncated}`; cap 500; 400 on invalid id. `GET /transcript/:spawnId/tool-cost` proxies to D2's `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs::aggregateByTool({projectDir, spawnId})`; **503 graceful fallback** when the D2 library isn't yet on disk (`{error: "tool-attribution library not yet available"}`) — D6 shipped before D2 in Wave 2 so this must not crash. Both routes exported for testability. **D6-T2**: `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` gains a collapsible `<details class="panel" id="tool-cost-panel">` sidebar panel — on viewer load fetches `/transcript/:id/tool-cost` and renders top-N tools by attributed tokens (tool name, calls, tokens, USD cost), debounced 2s refresh on SSE `turn_complete` / `result` frames, live-badge when SSE connection is open, graceful "tool attribution not yet wired" message on 503. **D6-T3**: every detached spawn from `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` prints a one-line banner `▶ Live transcript: http://127.0.0.1:{port}/transcript/{spawn-id}` — port from `ensureDashboardRunning().port` with `projectScopedDefaultPort(projectDir)` fallback; best-effort, never crashes the spawn. **D6-T4**: new `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-autostart.cjs` — `ensureDashboardRunning({projectDir, port?})` probes the port synchronously via a short-lived subprocess (`_isPortBusySync` uses `spawnSync` with `net.createServer().listen(port)` — host-less to match the server's IPv6-wildcard bind on macOS dual-stack); if free, fork-detaches the server with `spawn(…, {detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'})` + `child.unref()` + writes `.gsd-t/.dashboard.pid` (hyphen-removed; distinct from M38's `.gsd-t/dashboard.pid`). Idempotent: back-to-back calls don't double-spawn. Integrated at the top of `autoSpawnHeadless` so the banner printed immediately after is live. **D6-T5**: `.gsd-t/contracts/dashboard-server-contract.md` bumped to v1.2.0 — new §HTTP Endpoints entries for `/transcript/:id/tool-cost` and `/transcript/:id/usage` (with 503 fallback contract), new §Banner Format section (exact line shape + regex anchor), new §Autostart section (module contract + behavior + integration + non-goals). NOT recording `[benchmark-gate] FAIL` in progress.md — data is untrustworthy until stdout capture lands. Module exports injection points for unit tests — `orchestratorImpl` / `insessionImpl` / `spawnImpl` all overridable. Zero real `claude -p` spawns in the test — all injected. Full 3-run verdict remains an operator action (user invokes `gsd-t benchmark-orchestrator --runs 3` with real auth). **Wiring**: `bin/gsd-t.js` help now lists `benchmark-orchestrator`; the subcommand case spawnSyncs the driver with forwarded args (mirrors `orchestrate`). 1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to GSD-T are documented here. Updated with each release.
4	
5	## [3.15.10] - 2026-04-20
6	
7	### Added — Universal Token Capture Across GSD-T (M41)
8	
9	Every subagent spawn across GSD-T now routes through a single shared wrapper, retiring the silent `| N/A |` Tokens convention that preceded M41. Every spawn's input/output/cache tokens and cost USD land in both the human-readable `.gsd-t/token-log.md` and the machine-readable `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (schema v1, reused from M40 D4).
10	
11	**Token-capture wrapper (D1)**: `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` exports `captureSpawn({command, step, model, description, projectDir, spawnFn, domain?, task?})` and `recordSpawnRow({...})`. Parses bare + `.result`-wrapped + stream-json envelopes with assistant-vs-result precedence. Missing usage renders `—`, never `0`, never `N/A`. Migration-in-place upgrades existing `.gsd-t/token-log.md` to the canonical 12-column header (adds Tokens + Compacted columns).
12	
13	**Command-file doc-ripple (D2)**: all 20 spawn-capable `commands/*.md` files converted from inline `T_START=$(date +%s)` bash blocks to `captureSpawn`/`recordSpawnRow` pattern. `templates/CLAUDE-global.md` and the project `CLAUDE.md` carry the Token Capture Rule (MANDATORY). A canonical-block drift-guard test (`test/m41-canonical-block-drift.test.js`) asserts no legacy blocks remain and every OBSERVABILITY LOGGING declaration pairs with a wrapper call.
14	
15	**Historical backfill (D3)**: `bin/gsd-t-token-backfill.cjs` + `gsd-t backfill-tokens [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--patch-log] [--dry-run]`. Walks `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, `.gsd-t/stream-feed/*.jsonl`, and `.gsd-t/headless-*.log`. Handles both event-stream frames and stream-json frames. Idempotent via `source: "backfill"` key-tuple tracking. `--patch-log` atomically rewrites legacy `N/A`/`0`/`—` Tokens cells in place using tmp+rename.
16	
17	**Token dashboard (D4)**: `bin/gsd-t-token-dashboard.cjs` + `gsd-t tokens [--since] [--milestone] [--format table|json]`. Streams JSONL via `readline.createInterface`; aggregates byDay/byCommand/byModel; top-10 spawns by cost desc; cache-hit rate per model; rolling 7-day projection (daily avg × 30). Injects a 3-line token block at the tail of `gsd-t status`. Perf gate: 22ms on 10k-line JSONL (budget 500ms).
18	
19	**Enforcement (D5)**: `bin/gsd-t-capture-lint.cjs` + `gsd-t capture-lint [--staged|--all]`. Greps for `Task({`, `spawn('claude', ...)`, and `claude -p` without a surrounding `captureSpawn`/`recordSpawnRow` within ±20 lines. Balanced-quote heuristic excludes JS-string-literal false positives. Whitelists: wrapper/linter modules themselves, `test/**`, `commands/gsd-t-help.md`, comment-only lines, markdown prose outside fences, any line with `GSD-T-CAPTURE-LINT: skip` marker nearby. Opt-in pre-commit hook via `gsd-t init --install-hooks` — appends idempotently to `.git/hooks/pre-commit` with a `# GSD-T capture lint` marker; never overwrites existing hooks.
20	
21	Tests: +27 net (1479/1479 total). No new contracts — reuses M40's `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 and `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0.
22	
23	## [3.14.10] - 2026-04-20
24	
25	### Added — External Task Orchestrator + Streaming Watcher UI (M40)
26	
27	JS orchestrator (`bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js`) drives `claude -p` one task per spawn: short-lived, fresh context, architecturally compaction-free. Benchmark gate PASS: 226s orchestrator vs 316s in-session on 20-task/3-wave/4-domain fixture (0.72× wall-clock, threshold 1.05×).
28	
29	**Orchestrator core (D1)**: wave-barrier join, per-wave Promise.all parallelism (default 3, ceiling 15 per Team Mode §15), workerPid attribution, SIGINT handler, retry policy per completion-signal-contract (first FAIL → single retry; second FAIL → halt wave), state.json atomic writes, task-boundary + wave-boundary synthetic frames emitted to stream-feed clients.
30	
31	**Task brief builder (D2)**: `bin/gsd-t-task-brief.js` composes 2–5 KB self-contained per-task briefs from `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md` + named contract excerpts + stack rules + Done Signal section; drop-order compactor guarantees non-droppable sections always survive.
32	
33	**Completion protocol (D3)**: `bin/gsd-t-completion-check.cjs` `assertCompletion()` returns `{ok, missing[], details}` by checking commit-on-expected-branch + progress.md entry + test exit. Ambiguous tasks (commit present but no progress entry) are flagged for operator triage — never silently claimed done.
34	
35	**Stream-feed server (D4)**: `scripts/gsd-t-stream-feed-server.js` — HTTP POST /ingest, WebSocket /feed?from=N replay, 127.0.0.1:7842, JSONL persist-before-broadcast. `scripts/gsd-t-token-aggregator.js` parses assistant + result envelope usage and writes `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` schema v1 + rewrites `.gsd-t/token-log.md` in place. New CLI: `gsd-t stream-feed`.
36	
37	**Stream-feed UI (D5)**: `scripts/gsd-t-stream-feed.html` — 47.5 KB, zero-dep, zero-token-cost local dashboard. Dark-mode claude.ai-style continuous feed with task/wave banners (duration + cost/tokens chips), token corner bar (running total), localStorage-persisted filters (tasks/domains/waves), auto-scroll pause + "↓ Jump to live" button.
38	
39	**Recovery and resume (D6)**: `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-recover.cjs` `recoverRunState()` reconciles interrupted runs via assertCompletion replay; `--resume` + `--no-archive` flags on `gsd-t orchestrate`; `/gsd-t-resume` Step 0.3 auto-detects in-flight state.json and surfaces resume invocation; 24 recovery unit tests cover fresh/terminal/resume modes + ambiguous classification + PID liveness.
